name: TERT mRNA protected human skin systems from radiation DNA damage without telomere extension
summary: TERT mRNA is relevant telomerase-therapy boundary context, but it is radiation-injury biology rather than aging efficacy.
statement: TERT mRNA pretreatment reduced radiation-induced DNA damage, mitochondrial ROS, and apoptosis in human primary skin cells and tissues, but did not extend telomere length during the experiment.
population_or_model: Human primary skin cells and human skin-tissue experimental systems exposed to ionizing radiation
quantitative_note: The abstract reports reduced DNA damage and no telomere-length extension during the experimental period, but does not provide an aging or clinical-outcome result.
caveats: Radiation-injury protection is not direct evidence for broad aging or telomere restoration.
The no-telomere-extension result makes this boundary context rather than support for telomere-length rescue.
The PubMed conflict statement reports patents and a company relationship around mRNA telomerase technology.
